summary: When verify reports pybind wheel-build failure with thin stdout, reproduce CppExtension build separately before rewriting bindings; if standalone build passes, treat it as a transient packaging issue and continue validating the actual kernel/interface changes.

For fused ConvTranspose2d patterns, remember torch.nn.ConvTranspose2d includes its own conv bias by default. If the custom kernel only consumes weight plus an external subtract-bias, outputs drift globally; add conv_transpose.bias as a separate input and accumulate it before the fused subtract+tanh.

If a later build failure appears after introducing fused activation buffers, verify against the final passing version and keep the interface synchronized: project_json, host shape checks, kernel inputs, python_bind signature, and ModelNew forward arguments must all be updated together when a new fused input like conv_bias is added.
